repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery Sport D165 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Revised design means this is much less common
Replacement often £3,500–£5,800 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Discovery Sport D165? Check These First
buyIf: * 2019+ registered (post-facelift) with the revised chain tensioner design
* Full Land Rover service history with correct 0W-20 C5 oil specification
* Documented EGR cooler and AdBlue system history (any replacements logged)
* Regular long journeys or documented DPF regeneration pattern
avoidIf: * Any evidence of coolant loss or white smoke (EGR cooler failure imminent)
* Service history missing or using incorrect oil specification
* AdBlue warning light showing without a documented fix
* Short-journey-only history with DPF warnings present

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 2019+ Discovery Sport D165 is a significantly improved version of the Ingenium diesel. The timing chain tensioner revision, improved DPF logic, and 48V MHEV system make this one of the most refined and reliable variants of the 204DTD family. Buy on history, maintain the EGR and AdBlue systems, and the D165 will serve you well.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery Sport D165 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery Sport D165 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle; post-2019 should be quiet — if not, investigate timing chain
* Service History — Must show 0W-20 C5 oil; Land Rover schedule is 21,000 miles but 12,000 is safer
* Coolant Level — Check for loss — EGR cooler leaks are common on this engine
* Oil Level — Check for rising oil level (diesel contamination from DPF regeneration)
* DPF Regeneration — Ask about driving patterns; frequent short trips cause DPF issues
* AdBlue System — Any warnings? Check AdBlue level and age
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? NOx sensors are a common culprit
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a Land Rover Discovery Sport D165 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Discovery Sport D165 engine (204DTD MHEV) typically costs £3,500–£5,800 supply only. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,400. These are specialist-only replacements — the Ingenium diesel requires Land Rover specialist expertise.

items.question: Is the Land Rover Discovery Sport D165 reliable?
items.answer: The 2019+ D165 is the improved generation of the Ingenium diesel. JLR revised the timing chain tensioners and DPF regeneration logic for the L550 facelift. While EGR cooler leaks and AdBlue sensor issues remain common, the fundamental engine is now more robust. The 48V MHEV system adds complexity but has proven generally reliable.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Discovery Sport D165?
items.answer: EGR cooler coolant leaks (£600–£1,200 repair), oil dilution from DPF regeneration (requires regular oil changes), and AdBlue/NOx sensor failures (£500–£1,500 repair). The pre-2019 timing chain tensioner issues are significantly less common on the D165.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Discovery Sport D165 engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Discovery Sport D165 values range from £12,000–£30,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £4,500–£5,800 reconditioned engine replacement represents 15–50% of vehicle value — comfortably within our 60% Land Rover threshold. For most examples, replacement is financially viable.

items.question: How do I find my Land Rover Discovery Sport D165 engine code?
items.answer: The D165 uses the 204DTD engine code — a 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium diesel with 48V mild-hybrid. This is the same base engine as the D150, D180, D200 and D240, differentiated by ECU tuning. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the EGR cooler issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — EGR cooler replacement (£600–£1,200) is a straightforward repair on the 204DTD. Ignoring coolant loss can lead to overheating and potential head gasket failure. At the first sign of coolant loss or white smoke, book an inspection immediately.
